generated_comment: This is exactly why decent families don't want their sons to work in this industry. These actresses know very well that the man is married and still they use their charm to trap them — and then the poor wife and kids suffer at home. The men are also wrong but at the end of the day it is always these women who initiate and create the situation. My wife agrees, yeh sab predatory behavior hai. Shame on her. 🙏
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes the 'women as seductresses' trope by framing the actress as the calculated aggressor who 'traps' men, while simultaneously softening male culpability ('men are also wrong but…'). This reflects covert misogyny because it presents itself as a balanced moral judgment while systematically assigning primary blame and agency for the affair to the woman, reinforcing the homewrecker stereotype that holds women uniquely responsible for male marital fidelity.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Bollywood news website's Facebook page after a story broke that a famous actress was allegedly romantically involved with her married co-star. The comments section is full of people attacking the actress. You are adding your comment about what the actress's behavior says about her character and what women in the film industry are like in general.
